name: Ripples (2020)
description: Analog multimode filter. HP, BP, LP outputs with switchable 2/4-pole slope on BP/LP. Quad-VCA core with OTA resonance, LP through a final VCA, soft-clipping drive on input 1 — Japanese-classic voicing.

controls.name: FREQ
controls.description: Cutoff frequency. Full audio sweep from sub to treble.
controls.detail: 20Hz – 20kHz · CV summed with FM and V/OCT inputs

controls.name: Slope switch
controls.description: Toggles between 12 dB/oct (2-pole) and 24 dB/oct (4-pole) response on the BP and LP outputs. HP output is unaffected.
controls.detail: Down: 2-pole · Up: 4-pole · HP slope tracks resonance

controls.name: RES
controls.description: Resonance amount. Self-oscillation occurs around 75% of rotation upward. Loudness-compensated so level doesn't drop as resonance rises.
controls.detail: 0 – 12 o'clock: clean · ~75% onward: self-oscillation · liquid coloration near the edge

controls.name: IN 1 GAIN
controls.description: Input 1 pre-gain into the soft-clip stage. Low = clean, high = overdrive. Modular-level signals saturate at full; line-level sources find unity here.

controls.name: FM attenuverter
controls.description: Bipolar attenuverter for the FM CV input. Centre zeroes the FM path.
controls.detail: CCW: invert · noon: zero · CW: unity · ±depth for FM input

inputs.name: IN 1
inputs.description: Audio input through the soft-clipping drive stage. Gain set by IN 1 GAIN knob. Mixed internally with IN 2.
inputs.voltage: modular to line level · overdrives at high gain

inputs.name: IN 2
inputs.description: Clean audio input matching the tonal character of Ripples mkI. Always unity gain, no drive. Mixed internally with IN 1.

inputs.name: FM
inputs.description: Frequency modulation CV, scaled by the FM attenuverter. Non-tracking — use V/OCT for pitch.

inputs.name: V/OCT
inputs.description: 1V/octave tracking cutoff CV. Trimmer-calibrated for ~4 octaves of musical response when self-oscillating. Improved tracking over Ripples mkI.

inputs.name: RES CV
inputs.description: CV input for resonance amount. Adds to front-panel RES knob.

inputs.name: VCA CV
inputs.description: CV input for the final VCA on the LP output. 0V silences, +5V gives unity, above +5V is progressively compressed. Normalled to +7V when unpatched.

outputs.name: HP
outputs.description: High-pass output. Slope shifts with resonance (not affected by the slope switch). Quad-VCA core gives a bright but not harsh character.

outputs.name: BP
outputs.description: Band-pass output. Slope (2-pole or 4-pole) follows the front-panel switch.

outputs.name: LP
outputs.description: Low-pass output through the internal VCA. Slope follows the switch. This is the primary voice output.

behaviors.name: Two input paths
behaviors.description: IN 1 feeds a soft-clip drive stage (GAIN-scaled) for coloured overdrive; IN 2 is a clean path that matches the tone of Ripples mkI. Both are summed internally — patch a clean source to IN 2 and drive a second source through IN 1 for blended parallel processing on a single filter.

behaviors.name: Switchable slope (BP/LP only)
behaviors.description: 2-pole (12 dB/oct) is punchy and present, 4-pole (24 dB/oct) is rounder and more classic-LP. HP output ignores the switch and its slope instead moves with resonance — useful for resonant whistles that stay out of the way of a simultaneous LP voice.

behaviors.name: Loudness-compensated resonance
behaviors.description: Unlike most ladder designs, the OTA feedback stage keeps perceived loudness flat as resonance rises — no loss of body at high Q. The compensation introduces a slight liquid coloration at the resonant peak, intentionally evoking Japanese 80s filters (JP-8, Polysix).

behaviors.name: Clean self-oscillation sine
behaviors.description: A soft-limiter OTA in the feedback path keeps self-oscillation clean — the LP output emits a pure sine wave above 75% resonance. With V/OCT patched, Ripples works as a sine-wave VCO over about four octaves of musical tracking.

behaviors.name: Improved V/OCT tracking over mkI
behaviors.description: The 2020 revision has dedicated, trimmer-calibrated V/OCT with ~4 octaves of musical tracking — the mkI had no V/OCT input at all. Set FREQ ~10 o'clock, RES to max, dial the trimmer while playing octaves on a calibrated keyboard (filter is not temperature-stabilised, expect drift on long sessions).

behaviors.name: LP VCA always open
behaviors.description: VCA CV is normalled to +7V internally, so the LP output is always open by default. Patching any CV breaks the normal. Great when you want the filter to double as the VCA for a voice — one envelope replaces a separate VCA module.

behaviors.name: VCA soft-compression overhead
behaviors.description: The VCA on LP is linear up to +5V (unity gain). Above +5V, additional voltage is progressively compressed rather than linearly scaling — so a hot envelope peaking at +8V doesn't overdrive the output. Useful headroom when patching raw envelope modules into VCA CV.

behaviors.name: IN 1 as overdriving preamp
behaviors.trigger: IN 1 GAIN near max · clean source
behaviors.description: The IN 1 soft-clip stage with gain up to 5× serves as a line-level-to-modular preamp with built-in saturation. A guitar or synth output patched to IN 1 gets amplified and overdriven before filtering, giving a vintage drive character that's independent of filter resonance.

patchIdeas.name: Clean voice + drive send
patchIdeas.patch: VCO saw → IN 2 (clean path) · same VCO → IN 1 via attenuator → IN 1 GAIN high (drive path) · envelope → VCA CV · slope to 4-pole · play with the IN 1 attenuator to blend clean and overdriven tones through one filter without leaving Ripples.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ripples (2020):
* Slope: 4-pole

VCO:
Envelope:
Attenuator:

- VCO (Saw) -> Ripples (2020) (IN 2)
- VCO (Saw) -> Attenuator (In)
- Attenuator (Out) -> Ripples (2020) (IN 1)
- Envelope (Out) >> Ripples (2020) (VCA CV)
- Ripples (2020) (LP) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Self-oscillating sine VCO
patchIdeas.patch: No audio input · RES full CW · V/OCT from sequencer · FREQ sets octave · LP out is a pure sine · use Ripples as a second VCO for kick drums or sub layers · VCA CV from envelope to gate the sine on and off.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ripples (2020):
* RES: full CW (self-oscillating)
* Slope: 4-pole

Sequencer:
Envelope:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Ripples (2020) (V/OCT)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> Ripples (2020) (VCA CV)
- Ripples (2020) (LP) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Parallel HP + LP split
patchIdeas.patch: Drum loop → IN 2 · HP → stereo L (transients, hats, cymbals) · LP → stereo R (body, kick) · independent effects on each bus · slope to 2-pole so both bands stay wide · the HP's resonance-dependent slope lets RES sculpt how much mid leaks into the HP channel.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ripples (2020):
* Slope: 2-pole
* RES: ~11 o'clock

- Drum Loop (Out) -> Ripples (2020) (IN 2)
- Ripples (2020) (HP) -> Reverb L (In)
- Ripples (2020) (LP) -> Delay R (In)

patchIdeas.name: Ping filter with no audio
patchIdeas.patch: No audio input · RES around 70% (just below self-oscillation) · envelope → FM input with FM attenuverter at 2 o'clock · trigger pings the filter to produce plucked, tuned percussion · V/OCT for pitch per step · short envelope decay sharpens the ping.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ripples (2020):
* RES: ~70%
* FM attenuverter: ~2 o'clock

Sequencer:
Envelope:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Ripples (2020) (V/OCT)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> Ripples (2020) (FM)
- Ripples (2020) (LP) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Filter-FM cross-modulation
patchIdeas.patch: VCO1 → IN 2 as carrier · VCO2 audio-rate → FM input · FM attenuverter controls depth · self-oscillating RES adds sideband colour · slope to 4-pole for heavier spectrum · unorthodox FM voice that blends filter resonance with oscillator modulation.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ripples (2020):
* Slope: 4-pole
* RES: high

VCO 1:
VCO 2:

- VCO 1 (Saw) -> Ripples (2020) (IN 2)
- VCO 2 (Sine) >> Ripples (2020) (FM) // audio-rate FM
- Ripples (2020) (LP) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: No-VCA voice
patchIdeas.patch: VCO → IN 2 · envelope directly to VCA CV (unpatched = +7V, so patching gates the voice) · skip an external VCA entirely · the LP output's internal VCA handles both filter and amp stages · great for space-constrained racks or a simple synth voice.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ripples (2020):
* Slope: 4-pole
* RES: ~noon

VCO:
Envelope:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> Envelope (Trig)
- VCO (Saw) -> Ripples (2020) (IN 2)
- Envelope (Out) >> Ripples (2020) (VCA CV) // replaces VCA
- Ripples (2020) (LP) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Line-level guitar preamp
patchIdeas.patch: Guitar → IN 1 · IN 1 GAIN at 3 o'clock for overdrive · slow envelope follower → FM with attenuverter at 1 o'clock · played harder opens the filter (auto-wah) · LP with light resonance to shape · LP VCA held open with nothing patched.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ripples (2020):
* IN 1 GAIN: ~3 o'clock
* Slope: 4-pole

Guitar:
Env Follower:

- Guitar (Out) -> Ripples (2020) (IN 1)
- Guitar (Out) -> Env Follower (In)
- Env Follower (Out) >> Ripples (2020) (FM) // auto-wah
- Ripples (2020) (LP) -> Output (In)
